title: UP 280% YTD, Will AMD’s Rally Continue?
detail:
UP 280% YTD, Will AMD’s Rally Continue?
Vandita Jadeja
Thu, June 18, 2026 at 11:44 AM CDT 4 min read
AMD
NVDA
META
ORCL
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

AMD earns a BUY rating with a $561.83 price target, offering 11% upside after surging 137% year to date.

AMD's trailing P/E of 169 dwarfs NVIDIA's 31, yet hyperscaler commitments from OpenAI, Meta, and Oracle underpin the bull case.

China export restrictions cost AMD $440 million in FY25, and insider selling across 87 recent transactions signals the primary bear-case risk.

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and AMD didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

The 24/7 Wall St. price target for Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) is $561.83 over the next 12 months. With AMD trading at $507.29 after a brutal 7.3% single-day reset on June 16, our proprietary model still points to roughly 10.75% of upside from here.

Advanced Micro Devices

Our recommendation is buy, with a confidence level of 90%. The rally has further to run, though the easiest gains are behind us.

24/7 Wall St. Price Target Summary

Metric

	

Value




Current Price

	

$507.29




24/7 Wall St. Price Target

	

$561.83




Upside

	

10.75%




Recommendation

	

BUY




Confidence Level

	

90%

From $214 to $507: The Rally That Refused to Quit

AMD has gained 136.87% year to date and 301.37% over the past 12 months, climbing from a 52-week low of $125.77 to within striking distance of the $558.37 high.

The catalyst was Q1 FY2026, reported May 5: revenue of $10.25 billion grew 37.85% YoY, non-GAAP EPS of $1.37 beat by 5.88%, and the Data Center segment surged 57% to $5.78 billion. Free cash flow expanded 252.96% YoY. Since the filing, AMD has rallied 47.8% against just 2.2% for the S&P 500.

AMD earnings explorer
The Case for $600+

Bulls have a credible path to our $603.94 bull-case target. Lisa Su told investors that "customer engagement around MI450 Series and Helios is strengthening, with leading customer forecasts exceeding our initial expectations." Behind that statement sit the OpenAI 6-gigawatt deployment, Meta's 6-gigawatt Instinct commitment, and Oracle's 50,000-GPU Helios supercluster launching in Q3 2026.

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and AMD didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Q2 guidance of $11.2 billion implies 46% YoY growth, with non-GAAP gross margin expanding to 56%. AMD's recent MLPerf Training 6.0 results showed a 3.5X generational improvement on Llama 2-70B, narrowing the perceived gap with NVIDIA's (NASDAQ:NVDA) B200. Of 51 covering analysts, 41 rate AMD a buy with zero sell calls.

What Could Go Wrong

The bear case targets $427.24, a roughly 15.78% drawdown. Three risks dominate. First, valuation: AMD trades at a trailing P/E of 169 versus NVIDIA at 31, even though NVIDIA posts 65.6% operating margins compared to AMD's 14.4%.

title: Meta Platforms (META) Stock After AI Investment Surge And Recent Pullback
detail:
Meta Platforms (META) Stock After AI Investment Surge And Recent Pullback
Bailey Pemberton
Thu, June 18, 2026 at 11:20 AM CDT 7 min read
META
Trade META on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

If you are wondering whether Meta Platforms stock still offers value after a strong multi year run, the latest signals around price and valuation give you plenty to think about.

The share price closed at US$567.58, with the stock down 0.6% over the past week, down 7.1% over the past month, down 12.7% year to date and still up 103.3% over three years and 68.1% over five years, which can change how investors think about both upside and risk.

Recent headlines have focused on Meta Platforms' ongoing investments in areas like artificial intelligence, virtual and augmented reality, and its broader family of social platforms. Together these continue to shape how investors think about its long term business profile. Other news has also highlighted regulatory and competitive pressures in digital advertising and social media that may help explain recent share price moves.

Simply Wall St currently assigns Meta Platforms a valuation score of 4 out of 6, which suggests some checks flag the stock as potentially undervalued and others do not. The next step is to compare how different valuation approaches assess the shares and then return at the end to an even broader way of thinking about what the stock might be worth.

Find out why Meta Platforms's -18.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: Meta Platforms Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what Meta Platforms stock could be worth by projecting future cash flows and discounting them back to today in dollar terms. In this case, a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model is used, which focuses on cash available to shareholders after expenses and investments.

Meta Platforms currently reports trailing twelve month free cash flow of about $64.5b. Analyst and extrapolated estimates suggest projected free cash flow of about $78.0b by 2030, with a series of annual projections in between that are discounted back to present value. Simply Wall St uses analyst inputs for earlier years, then extends the trend beyond the explicit forecast period.

Adding up those discounted cash flows produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $695.59 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $567.58, the DCF output indicates the stock trades at about an 18.4% discount, which suggests Meta Platforms may be undervalued on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Meta Platforms is undervalued by 18.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 48 more high quality undervalued stocks.

title: Tim Cook Says Apple Is About to Get Swamped By a “100-Year Flood”
detail:
Tim Cook Says Apple Is About to Get Swamped By a “100-Year Flood”
Rich Duprey
Thu, June 18, 2026 at 10:13 AM CDT 4 min read
AAPL
NVDA
AMZN
MSFT
GOOG
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

Tim Cook called surging DRAM and NAND costs a "hundred-year flood," with the iPhone 18 Pro potentially rising to $1,299 from $1,099.

Apple's 2.5 billion active devices and $99 billion in annual free cash flow give it pricing power most companies simply don't have.

The real threat isn't sticker shock but longer upgrade cycles, as price hikes in the range of $100 to $200 may convince users to keep older devices longer.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

The artificial intelligence boom is reshaping more than just software. It is rewriting the economics of the entire technology industry. The four largest hyperscalers -- Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG), and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) -- are expected to spend roughly $750 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026. That spending spree is consuming enormous amounts of memory and storage chips, pushing prices higher across the supply chain.

Mario Tama / Getty Images News via Getty Images

For years, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) largely absorbed those costs. Now, CEO Tim Cook says the company has reached its limit. The question for investors is whether higher prices will slow Apple sales or simply reinforce the company's pricing power.

Staring Down a Tsunami of Rising Costs

Cook told The Wall Street Journal that rising memory and storage costs have become "unsustainable" and that price increases are now unavoidable. He compared the chip shortage to a "hundred-year flood," saying he had never seen anything like it in more than 40 years in the industry.

Apple's costs are being squeezed by surging demand for DRAM and NAND chips from AI data centers, which are soaking up production from suppliers such as Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), Samsung Electronics, and SK hynix.

The issue has been building for months. During Apple's April earnings call, Cook warned that memory costs would become an "increasing impact" on the business throughout 2026. Analysts now expect Macs and iPads to see price increases before the launch of the iPhone 18 lineup this fall. Some estimates suggest the iPhone 18 Pro could eventually cost as much as $1,299, up from $1,099 today.

24/7 Wall St.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

Big Tech is gobbling up the world's chip supply, and Tim Cook warns the rising costs have reached a breaking point. Your next iPhone could cost $200 more, but Apple’s massive ecosystem suggests they can get away with it. © 24/7 Wall St.

title: The Case for and Against Buying SpaceX Right Now
detail:
The Case for and Against Buying SpaceX Right Now
Steven Porrello, The Motley Fool
Thu, June 18, 2026 at 9:20 AM CDT 4 min read
SPCX
META
NVDA
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

The ability to calculate wisely in the midst of strong emotions -- fear, greed, excitement, worry -- is one of the hallmark habits of a seasoned investor. To look for the real company beneath the public face, the core of its operations beneath promises, the essential nature of the business -- this kind of clear-eyed analysis, more than anything else, is what's needed in a market as richly valued as it is in 2026.

This is as true for small companies and start-ups as it is for members of the trillion-dollar club, like the newly minted Space Exploration Technologies (NASDAQ: SPCX). Now that the most highly anticipated IPO since Facebook (now Meta Platforms) is over, let's look closely at SpaceX's pros and cons.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Image source: Getty Images.
The bull case: Rockets, AI, and the internet beamed from space

SpaceX is essentially three businesses: space technology, connectivity, and artificial intelligence (AI). This trinity of tech does not have equal parts, however, and it appears that the most profitable one is connectivity.

In 2025, the connectivity segment (driven mostly by Starlink) generated over $11 billion in revenue -- roughly three-fifths of SpaceX's total -- and left the company with over $4 billion in operating income. Starlink currently has over 10 million subscribers across more than 160 countries, a number it claims will grow to 25 million by the end of 2026.

The other two businesses -- space and AI -- together generated just over $7 billion of revenue, underscoring the dominance of connectivity. Here's another figure that stresses it: Between 2023 and 2025, total revenue grew at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 34%. But connectivity? That segment grew at a whopping 71% CAGR.

Connectivity's near-term total addressable market (TAM) -- according to figures from SpaceX's IPO roadshow -- is $1.6 trillion, which was roughly the company's opening valuation. Together with space and AI, SpaceX claims it can address an enormous $6 trillion market opportunity, about three times more than its current valuation.

The bear case: A remarkable company with a sky-high valuation

The bull case for SpaceX is its potential to become deeply embedded in infrastructure, with all three businesses working together to make it the defining company of the century.
